The applicable interest rate on a home loan consists of two components, the base rate and the markup rate. The combination of the two is what you will be paying on the loan. Let's explore these components to give you a better understanding. Base Rate - It is the standard lending rate of the bank, applicable for all retail loans. This rate is subject to Types of Interest Rates in Home Loan There are mainly two types of home loan interest rates charged by most banks. 1. Fixed Interest Rate: In this system of computation, the rate remains even throughout the loan tenor. There will be no change in the interest charges since the rate remains fixed. Depending on the offer, you may be allowed to switch over to the floating rate system after completing  How to Apply for a Home Loan? Step 1: Complete the Home Loan Application Form Commence the home loan application process by filling out the application form. Provide the following details: Personal information of the applicant (name, contact number, etc.) Current residential address Monthly income Educational background Current and previous employment details Details of the property for which the  Determining Factors for Your Home Loan Interest Rate When applying for a home loan, it's important to understand the factors that lenders consider while assessing your eligibility and determining the interest rate. Since home loans involve substantial amounts, banks and Housing Finance Companies (HFCs) conduct a thorough evaluation of your credit history, repayment capacity, income, and property d
features: Base Rate - It is the standard lending rate of the bank, applicable for all retail loans. This rate is subject to frequent changes on the basis of multiple inputs.
Markup - This component of a small percentage is added to the base rate to arrive at the EIR (Effective interest rate) for a specific type of home loan and varies from one type to another.
Effective Interest Rate (EIR) = Base Rate + Markup

Advantage: Since the rate remains fixed, you know how much interest charges you’re paying upfront. Your loan will be shielded from frequent rate fluctuations and will save money in a the long run if there is a hike in lending rates.
Disadvantage: If the standard lending rates fall, you will not benefit since the interest component remains frozen.
Advantage: The most visible perk of opting for the floating rate is that you have the advantage of being billed on the basis of the latest rate. If the rates fall, you save on interest charges.
Disadvantage: In rare scenarios, if the standard rates go up, the loan has to be bear the brunt of being billed a higher rate.
Personal information of the applicant (name, contact number, etc.)
Current residential address
Monthly income
Educational background
Current and previous employment details
Details of the property for which the loan is sought
Estimated property cost
The current method of financing a home

After submitting the form and required documents, the bank will process your application and conduct a thorough credit score check . Ensure accurate information submission, as any discrepancies can lead to rejection.

Upon verification of submitted documents, the bank sanctions the loan. This typically takes one day to two days, pending accurate paperwork. Occasionally, an in-person verification may be required for additional information.

Before disbursing the loan, the bank conducts legal and technical assessments. For under-construction properties, the construction progress and quality are assessed. Resale properties undergo scrutiny for age, maintenance, previous mortgages, and ownership rights.
After successful verification, the applicant pays the home loan processing fee, ranging from 0.25% to 0.50% of the loan amount, to maintain the loan account.

Upon meeting all eligibility criteria, the applicant receives a formal agreement letter, and the loan amount is disbursed to their bank account. A complete home loan EMI schedule is provided via email.
